heroHeadline: Iran Silent 24 Hours After Ceasefire — FIFA Congress in Vancouver 21 Days Away, 63 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: One day after a Pakistan-brokered US-Iran ceasefire removed the central obstacle to Iran's World Cup participation, Iran's government has issued no revised statement on Group G attendance — leaving FIFA's April 30 Congress in Vancouver as the formal decision point with 63 days to the June 11 opening match. Iran's Sports Minister had conditioned any participation reversal on 'security guarantees,' which the ceasefire now tentatively provides, but the Iran Football Federation has not withdrawn its relocation request. FIFA 'Canada Celebrates' launched today — a 38-stop national fan engagement programme reaching 75% of Canadians. US Soccer revealed its drone analytics and AI scouting infrastructure supporting Pochettino's World Cup prep; the final 26-man USMNT roster will be announced May 26 in NYC, with the May 31 friendly against Senegal the last competitive tune-up before the cutdown.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, Inside World Football
